title: CBN Increase Targeted Credit Facility To N300 Billion
description: In order to accommodate many more beneficiaries and boost consumer expenditure which should positively stimulate the economy, the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) have decided to double the funding of Ta
html:
In order to accommodate many more beneficiaries and boost consumer expenditure which should positively stimulate the economy, the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) have decided to double the funding of Targeted Credit Facility (TCF), to N300 billion. Targeted Credit is one of the several interventions of the bank aimed at reducing the negative impacts of Covid-19 on Nigerian..

The Governor of CBN Mr Godwin Emefiele disclosed this at the 30th Edition of Finance Correspondent Association of Nigerian (FICAN) and Business Editors Seminar that was held on Saturday March 20, 2021, with the Theme: “Leveraging The Digital Economy To Drive Growth, Job Creation And Sustainable Development”

Emefiele that was represented by CBN Deputy Governor Corporate Services Mr Edward Lamekek Adamu, in its opening address that was vitual, said “Given the resounding success of this program and its positive impact on output growth, we have decided to double this fund to about N300 billion”

Commenting on the impact of Covid-19, the apex bank governor noted that the CBN has equally made bold moves to alleviate the plight of households and and business owners and drive economic growth during the pandemic.

“We initially created a N150 billion Targeted Credit Facility (TCF) for affected households and small and medium enterprises through the NIRSAL Microfinance Bank. Already, N149.21 billion has been disbursed to 316,869 beneficiaries”
